channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CAF Reschedules WAFCON 2026 to July Amid Unforeseen Issues
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) has announced that the Women's Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON), originally set to kick off on March 17, 2026, in Morocco, has been postponed. The tournament ...

item.title: Trump Administration and Tech Giants Sign Nonbinding Pledge on Data Center Energy Costs Amid Skepticism
item.description: On March 4, 2026, President Donald Trump hosted representatives from major tech companies including Microsoft, Meta, OpenAI, xAI, Google/Alphabet, Oracle, and Amazon at the White House to sign a volun...

item.title: AU Leaders Discuss Continental Unity and Economic Independence Amid Global Unrest
item.description: During the recent African Union summit in Addis Ababa, leaders discussed ways to strengthen Africa's unity and independence in face of global instability. With tensions rising among Western countries ...

item.title: Plateau PDP Sets Up Caretaker Committee in Shendam LGA and Appoints New Chairman for Wase LGA
item.description: The Plateau State chapter of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) has taken decisive steps to reorganise its structure in two local government areas. Dem say dem appoint a nine-member caretaker committe...

item.title: Three Entrepreneurs with Disabilities Receive 17m/- for Winning Wazo Bora Pitch Competition
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, three entrepreneurs with disabilities have been awarded a total of 17 million Tanzanian shillings as seed capital after winning the Wazo Bora Pitch Competition. The contest featured ...

item.title: Manipuri Film 'Boong' Wins BAFTA, Paul Thomas Anderson’s 'One Battle After Another' Dominates Awards
item.description: Indian filmmaker Lakshmipriya Devi has made a remarkable entry into international cinema with her debut feature 'Boong', winning the Best Children’s & Family Film award at the 2026 BAFTAs. The Manipur...

item.title: Ten dead after Hyderabad firecracker factory explosion, several injured
item.description: In a tragic incident in Latifabad, Hyderabad, a firecracker factory blast has claimed the lives of ten people, while over a dozen others sustained injuries. The Civil Hospital Hyderabad confirmed the ...

item.title: Ghana Engineer Dies in Ambulance After Being Denied Admission at Three Hospitals Due to No Beds
item.description: A tragic incident in Ghana has once again brought attention to the serious problems in the country's healthcare system. On February 6, 2026, a 29-year-old engineer named Charles Amissah was involved i...

item.title: President Ruto Welcomes Three New Ambassadors to Kenya
item.description: President William Ruto of Kenya officially received the credentials of three newly appointed ambassadors at State House in Nairobi. The diplomats, Rulan Mahmoud Samara from Jordan, Jacinto Januario Ma...

item.title: Metrolinx CEO Apologises for Finch West LRT Disruptions and Commits to Improving Reliability and Communication
item.description: Metrolinx, Ontario's transit planning agency, is actively working to rebuild public confidence following the challenging early months of the Finch West Light Rail Transit (LRT) line. The 10-kilometre,...

item.title: How Modern Football Mirrors Wider Social Discontent
item.description: The article explores the growing sense of disillusionment surrounding modern football, highlighting how fans, pundits, and former players alike express frustration with aspects of the game such as VAR...

item.title: Killing of M23 Spokesman Willy Ngoma Threatens Congo Truce
item.description: Early morning strike in Rubaya killed Willy Ngoma, one of the most prominent faces of the M23 rebellion in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. This incident comes at a sensitive time when a fragile ...

item.title: Pakistan raises concerns on India-Canada uranium and nuclear cooperation
item.description: Islamabad has expressed serious concern regarding the recent uranium supply agreement between India and Canada, warning that it could impact regional stability and global nuclear non-proliferation eff...

item.title: Updates on US-Iran Conflict: Military Escalation and Australian Repatriation Flights
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have escalated following US military action against Iran, including the sinking of an Iranian warship by a US submarine. A second Iranian vessel was reported off Sri Lanka,...

item.title: Option Nyahunzvi Highlights Shona Culture and Traditions in 'Zvatiri' Exhibition
item.description: Option Dzikamai Nyahunzvi, a young Zimbabwean artist born in 1992, is presenting his latest solo exhibition called 'Zvatiri' (Who We Are) at the National Gallery of Zimbabwe. The exhibition celebrates...

item.title: Armed Men on Motorcycles Raid Village Near Woro in Kwara, Loot Phone Shops, No Casualties Reported
item.description: Suspected terrorists on motorcycles enter Gada Oli, one small village near Wawa and close to Woro for Kwara State, late Saturday night. Dem dey fire shots into the air to scatter people and make every...

item.title: Carlton's new strategy tested on opening night against Sydney
item.description: Carlton’s AFL season kicked off with a heavy defeat at the SCG, facing Sydney in the first round of 2026. After trading star forward Charlie Curnow to the Swans in October, Carlton made a series of ca...

item.title: Amit Shah to inaugurate NFSU campus and attend CISF Raising Day in Bhubaneswar
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah is scheduled to visit Bhubaneswar for a two-day programme starting Thursday evening. On Friday, he will lay the foundation for a permanent campus of the National Forensic...

item.title: VW Polo Vivo owners urged to inspect handbrake on 25,729 vehicles
item.description: The National Consumer Commission (NCC) has issued a warning to owners of VW Polo Vivo vehicles manufactured in 2025, following a manufacturer notification about a possible handbrake defect. A total of...

item.title: UK investigates over 60 cases of illness in babies linked to contaminated infant formula
item.description: The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) is investigating more than 60 cases of babies falling ill after consuming infant formula contaminated with the heat-resistant cereulide toxin. The contamination o...
